_comment: Reusable Touch Bar button template, following macOS's own native style: bordered:true with no custom background color gives the system's default gray-bezel look (same as the built-in 'esc' button) — clearly shows the tap region without a bold/colored CTA look. A fully borderless variant was tried first but made the touchable area impossible to see; a colored pill was tried and rejected as too loud. This bordered-gray style is the resolved default going forward. Copy this object into items.json, then: (1) generate an icon with ./icon-to-base64.sh, (2) paste it into image.base64, (3) set matchAppId to scope it to one app (or delete the line to always show), (4) wire up whatever actions you need.
